channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: Italy's Prysmian seals $1 bln buy of Channell to grow US presence
item.description: Prysmian has agreed to acquire U.S. connectivity device manufacturer Channell Commercial Corp for up to $1.15 billion to expand its presence in the United States. The deal was announced on Tuesday by the world's largest cable maker.

item.title: Recycled Copper in High-Voltage Cables
item.description: Prysmian has received notice to proceed for the Italy-Tunisia electrical interconnector project, which will utilize recycled copper in high-voltage cables. This initiative demonstrates Prysmian's commitment to sustainable copper solutions for grid infrastructure projects.

item.title: The Aluminum Shock Hitting the Global Economy
item.description: Jean Simard of the Aluminium Association of Canada warns that blocked shipments and damaged infrastructure are driving prices higher for American buyers. The video discusses supply disruptions affecting the Middle East and the timeline for recovery.

item.title: Aluminum closed in on prices not seen since 2022 following Iranian attacks
item.description: Aluminum stocks including Alcoa and Century Aluminum saw significant gains as prices approached levels unseen since 2022, according to S&P Global. However, J.P. Morgan forecasts a surplus of around 200 kmt in 2025 due to reduced global demand growth.

item.title: Aluminum: Driving Efficiency and Sustainability Across All Vehicle Segments
item.description: Recycling aluminum uses approximately 95% less energy than primary production, resulting in 95% fewer carbon emissions. The article highlights aluminum's role in enhancing vehicle efficiency and sustainability across all segments.

item.title: Moving Aluminum Production Into the Future
item.description: Aluminum production is overdue for an upgrade, but sustainable technology upgrades are now available and already in use. The article emphasizes emerging innovations that enable greener aluminum manufacturing.

item.title: Steel fell to 3,036 CNY/T on July 1, 2026, down 0.43% from the previous day
item.description: Steel prices dropped 0.43% from the previous day to reach 3,036 CNY per ton on July 1, 2026. Over the past month, steel's price has fallen 4.83%, though it remains 0.20% higher than the same period last year[5].

item.title: Hot-rolled (HR) coil and plate prices continued to tick higher even as cold-rolled (CR) and coated prices came in unchanged
item.description: According to SMU's latest market check, hot-rolled coil and plate prices are rising while cold-rolled and coated steel prices remain stable[6]. This indicates a mixed trend in the current steel market with specific segments experiencing upward pressure.

item.title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop? Latest Hot Rolled Coil ...
item.description: Current steel market prices show Hot-Rolled Coil at $1,109/ton, Cold Rolled Steel at $1,293/ton, and Coated Steel at $1,380/ton[4]. The article questions whether these prices are poised to decrease following recent increases.

item.title: Iron and Steel Technology Roadmap
item.description: This roadmap outlines critical pathways for the steel industry to cut emissions by at least 50% by 2050, including hydrogen, carbon capture, bioenergy, and direct electrification. It recommends establishing long-term CO2 reduction signals, creating markets for near-zero emissions steel, and accelerating material efficiency to support the global transition.

item.title: Low-Carbon Production of Iron & Steel: Technology Options, Economic Assessment and Policy
item.description: This paper reviews global iron and steel production and assesses decarbonization technologies such as hydrogen injection, solid biomass substitution, and zero-carbon electricity. It finds that replacing fossil fuel injection with biofuels can reduce greenhouse gas emissions by up to 25%, with potential reductions of up to 58% if the biomass is carbon neutral.

item.title: Copper Prices Hit Record High After US Imposes Tariffs In ...
item.description: Copper prices reached a new record high after the US announced copper tariffs aligned with existing 50% tariffs on steel and aluminum. The announcement caused prices to surge 17%, hitting an all-time high of around $5.9 per barrel, with US copper trading at a record premium of $2,750 per ton[2].

item.title: LIVE: After Gold & Silver, Copper Prices Touch Record High ...
item.description: Copper is quietly powering the world's fastest transformations as electrification accelerates, and markets are taking notice of its rising value. The video highlights how copper prices are touching record highs amid growing demand for red metal in global energy transitions[9].

item.title: Copper fell to 6.06 USD/Lbs on July 1, 2026, down 2.13% from the previous day
item.description: Copper prices fell to $6.06 per pound on July 1, 2026, representing a 2.13% decline from the previous day. Over the past month, copper's price has dropped 8.85%, though it remains 17.78% higher than the same period last year[4].

item.title: Nickel Prices Ready to Rise as Supply Tightens in Fourth Quarter
item.description: The global nickel market is reaching a critical inflection point as supply discipline, sustained demand growth, and supportive government policies converge to potentially end prolonged range-bound pricing between $15,000-$15,800 per ton. Indonesia's strategic shift represents the most significant development, suggesting the sector may emerge from investor skepticism toward a more balanced market capable of supporting higher sustainable pricing levels[2].

item.title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Nickel fell to 16,246 USD/T on July 1, 2026, down 0.39% from the previous day, with a monthly decline of 15.45% despite still being 5.91% higher than the previous year[5]. This data reflects recent volatility in the nickel market as traders assess supply constraints and demand trends in the battery raw materials sector[5].

item.title: Acciaieria di Cogne, bilancio in rosso e futuro incerto
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali ha registrato una perdita di 60,9 milioni di euro nel 2025, in netto peggioramento rispetto all'utile di otto milioni dell'anno precedente. L'azienda affronta una situazione complessa dovuta a dazi statunitensi, concorrenza asiatica e rincari dell'energia, con sindacati CGIL e Fiom preoccupati per le prospettive future.

item.title: Alla Cogne Acciai Speciali scattano 13 settimane di cassa integrazione
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali ha comunicato l'attivazione di 13 settimane di cassa integrazione ordinaria per alcuni reparti dell'acciaieria, dal 24 febbraio al 25 maggio, coinvolgendo circa 750 dipendenti. La misura è dovuta alla crisi del settore automotive, ai dazi annunciati da Donald Trump su acciaio e alluminio, e all'incertezza del mercato dell'acciaio.

item.title: Prysmian Boosts Use of Recycled Copper in U.S. Amid Trade Tension
item.description: Prysmian is progressively incorporating recycled copper in the United States as Chinese purchasers withdraw from the scrap market, according to its CEO[5]. This strategic shift highlights how trade disputes between the U.S. and China are reshaping global commodity flows[5].

item.title: Prysmian Group - Wikipedia
item.description: Prysmian S.p.A. is a multinational company headquartered in Milan, Italy, specializing in electrical cables for energy and telecom sectors and optical fibres[4]. The company is recognized as a leading player in the copper wire and cable market operating in over 50 countries[4][8].

item.title: Copper Cables - Prysmian
item.description: Prysmian produces a range of copper telecoms cables designed for special applications and environments, particularly those requiring fire-resistance characteristics[9]. These cables support the company's role as the world leader in cable manufacturing and telecom solutions[7][9].

item.title: 3 technologies helping the aluminum industry decarbonize
item.description: Hydro's HalZero technology eliminates CO2 emissions during aluminum smelting by keeping carbon and chlorine in a closed loop, emitting only oxygen instead. Researchers in the U.S. are also developing innovations to remove metal impurities from recycled aluminum, enhancing its sustainability and expanding its applications.

item.title: Aluminum Technology Market Report 2025: Key Data & Innovations
item.description: The 2025 aluminum technology market is driven by advancements in recycling technologies, demand from automotive and aerospace sectors, and AI integration in production. The industry shows a strong commitment to reducing greenhouse gas emissions and improving overall sustainability.

item.title: Innovations in Aluminum Manufacturing: What's New in 2024?
item.description: Cutting-edge technologies like AI, IoT, and advanced robotics are reshaping the aluminum production landscape, driving efficiency and sustainability. These innovations represent the forefront of modern aluminum manufacturing practices.

item.title: Transforming the aluminium industry: How innovation, automation and sustainability are shaping the future
item.description: Sustainability is now a necessity for aluminium manufacturers, who are racing to reduce carbon emissions and minimize waste through innovation and automation. The industry is actively transforming to meet environmental challenges while maintaining production efficiency.

item.title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
item.description: The world's largest nickel mine in Indonesia, PT Weda Bay Nickel, has been notified to drastically cut its ore production quota from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons in 2026. The company plans to apply for a revision of this decision, which aims to boost global prices for Indonesia's key export commodity used in stainless steel and EV batteries[1].

item.title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
item.description: Aluminum prices fell up to 2.7% on the London Metal Exchange as traders reacted to reports that President Trump plans to roll back some import tariffs on aluminum and steel products. The commodity traded 2.3% lower at $3,030.00 a ton amid a broad cross-asset selloff and lower US stock futures.

item.title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Aluminum decreased to 3,224.00 USD/T, marking the lowest level since March 2026. Over the past four weeks, the commodity lost 12.28%, though it has still increased 25.28% over the last 12 months.

item.title: AI data centers' massive demand for aluminum is crushing the US aluminum industry
item.description: The US plans to build a new aluminum smelter in Oklahoma with a capacity of about 600,000 tons per year, nearly doubling current domestic production. Currently, only six smelting operations remain in the US, with just four actively producing approximately 670,000 tons in 2024, accounting for less than 1% of global output.

item.title: US Steel Costs Impacted by War, Trade Deals
item.description: Steel prices have not dropped despite the Iran war, with diesel prices increasing about 50% and steel pricing up near $1,100 as the market enters a nearly nine-month march higher. Analysts predict prices could reach $1,500 for hot-rolled coil, noting that steel pricing typically peaks in late spring or early summer[2].

item.title: Steel fell to 3036 CNY/T on July 2, 2026
item.description: Steel prices fell to 3036 CNY/T on July 2, 2026, down 0.33% from the previous day, with a 4.50% decline over the past month. This recent drop suggests a potential short-term reversal in the previously upward pricing trend[6].

item.title: Steel Market Update: Home
item.description: Hot-rolled coil and plate prices continued to tick higher while cold-rolled and coated prices remained unchanged in SMU's latest market check. This indicates selective upward pressure on specific steel products amid broader market stability[7].

item.title: Copper Price - Copper Rate in India Today Live on The Economic Times
item.description: Copper prices are surging globally due to geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and booming AI-driven demand, with the metal increasingly viewed as a strategic commodity for the AI era. In India, copper prices rose nearly 1% to Rs 1,089.20/kg, tracking international gains ahead of the US Federal Reserve's policy decision.

item.title: Copper Prices Hit Record High After US Imposes Tariffs In Commodity Corner
item.description: Copper prices reached a new record high after the US announced copper tariffs aligned with existing 50% tariffs on steel and aluminium, causing prices to spike 17% to an all-time high of around $5.9 a barrel. The US cabinet meeting proposed 50% tariffs on copper, which significantly pushed prices higher, while US copper continues to buy at a record premium of $2,750 a ton.

item.title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Copper fell to 6.09 USD/Lbs on July 2, 2026, down 0.57% from the previous day, with the price falling 6.05% over the past month despite still being 19.48% higher than the previous year. This data reflects recent market volatility amid ongoing supply and demand dynamics.

item.title: Cogne invests in Aosta plant
item.description: Italian stainless long steel producer Cogne Acciai Speciali is investing in the modernisation of its Aosta plant in Italy by revamping the cooling chamber of the continuous casting machine for special steel blooms[1]. This upgrade aims to enhance production efficiency for special steel products.

item.title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali acquires 65 percent of Inox
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali has completed the acquisition of a 65 percent stake in Italian stainless steel and nickel alloy recycler Com.steel Inox SpA for €24 million[2]. This upstream integration supports the company's circularity commitments and optimizes its supply chain.

item.title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali to acquire majority stake in its scrap supplier
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali signed a contract to acquire a 65 percent controlling interest in Com.Steel Inox, its stainless steel scrap business supplier[3]. The transaction is expected to conclude in the coming months and will improve the company's environmental footprint.

item.title: Cogne Acciai Speciali acquires a controlling stake in a scrap supplier
item.description: As a subsidiary of Taiwan's Walsin Lihwa, Cogne Acciai Speciali agreed to acquire 65% of Com.Steel Inox, one of its main scrap suppliers[4]. The deal is subject to regulatory approvals and will be completed as a share purchase in the coming months.

item.title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali launches green hydrogen pilot plant
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali has started operations at a pilot plant in Aosta, northern Italy, focused on green hydrogen technology[5]. This initiative marks a significant step in the company's transition toward sustainable steel manufacturing practices.

item.title: Italy's Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper ...
item.description: Milan-listed Prysmian, the world's largest cable maker, reported a 2.1% share increase amid expectations of stronger U.S. pricing and margins following new copper tariff adjustments. Analysts at Citi believe copper tariffs will support pricing, while the company's CEO stated that the firm is poised to gain from recent U.S. tariff changes on imported cables.

item.title: Decarbonizing Aluminum: Rolling Out a More Sustainable Sector
item.description: Increased aluminum recycling and decarbonization technology have reduced the carbon intensity of aluminum production by 43 percent, making sustainable aluminum price-competitive with traditional fossil-fuel-based options. The most effective method involves sourcing renewable electricity from hydro, solar, and wind power, with Apple leading upstream sustainability through a $60 million joint venture known as Elysis.

item.title: Update on the Aluminum Industry Response to Climate Change
item.description: The article examines past efforts to reduce GHG emissions and highlights current industry trends toward 'green' low CO2e aluminum, with companies using hydroelectric power to differentiate their metal as low-carbon output. To validate their claims, manufacturers are joining the Aluminium Stewardship Initiative (ASI) and obtaining certification for their sustainable processes.

item.title: Boom-Bust-Bounce: Understanding Nickel's Latest Price Cycle
item.description: Global nickel prices edged down 0.20% on July 2, 2026, settling at $16,273.31 per ton, despite a 2026 rebound driven by Indonesia's supply controls and rising EV demand. The market faces potential downside pressure if Indonesia expands its RKAB mining quotas to 360 million tonnes, though projections suggest prices could reach $25,000 if supply tightens.

item.title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
item.description: Global nickel prices edged down 0.20% today, settling at $16,273.31 per ton globally and ¥110,479 per ton in China. The live chart tracks battery-grade and stainless steel nickel pricing trends.

item.title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop? Latest Hot Rolled Coil
item.description: Current steel prices include Cold Rolled Steel at $1,347/ton, Coated Steel at $1,455/ton, and Steel Plate at $1,286/ton. Nucor's weekly Consumer Spot Price data is also referenced as a key market indicator.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today - Investing.com
item.description: The current price of Nickel futures is 16,512.25, with a previous close of 16,265.63, indicating a slight upward movement in the market. The daily trading range shows active participation as traders respond to recent supply tightening signals.

item.title: Tenaris acquires control of Italy-based Fast
item.description: Luxembourg-based Tenaris announced it has acquired control of Fast Srl, a Bergamo-based company manufacturing equipment for oil and gas extraction. This acquisition strengthens Tenaris's capabilities in the global steel pipe sector.

item.title: Aluminium Prices Are On The Rise As US Premium At Record High
item.description: Aluminum prices have gained 13% in 2025 and are trading at three-year highs on the LME, driven by China's prevention of overcapacity in metal production and supply concerns from boxside grades. The market deficit in Europe stands at 1.8 million tons, with demand continuing to grow, suggesting higher prices going forward.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Have Surged Nearly 90% Since the Iran War ...
item.description: Aluminum prices have surged nearly 90% following the Iran War, with Kaiser Aluminum beating EPS estimates by 90.49%. Metal Exchange prices recently exceeded previous highs, reflecting strong market momentum amid geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Aluminum Market Trends - S&P Global
item.description: Extruders remain cautious for 2026 after an up-and-down 2025, with trade uncertainty and supply tightness pushing the Midwest Premium above $1/lb. The report highlights ongoing market volatility and supply constraints affecting aluminum pricing trends.

item.title: Aluminum closed in on prices not seen since 2022 following Iranian ...
item.description: Aluminum prices closed near levels not seen since 2022 following Iranian attacks on two Middle Eastern producers over the weekend. The geopolitical escalation has triggered significant price increases in the global aluminum market.

item.title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
item.description: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs on aluminum. This move reflects a strategic response to current market pressures and policy changes affecting the US aluminum industry.

item.title: Aluminum Rally Builds as War Risks Threaten Global Supply Chains
item.description: Middle East disruptions, constrained global production, and China's capacity limits are reshaping the aluminum market. China has imposed a self-imposed cap of 45.5 million tons, which it is expected to reach this year.

item.title: Aluminum hits four-year high on Mideast supply loss
item.description: Aluminum prices have reached a four-year high due to supply losses in the Middle East, with deficits expected to persist through 2026 and 2027. The market is projected to remain unbalanced until 2028.

item.title: Company targets extra 75,000 tonnes of annual primary aluminium production by end of 2026
item.description: A company is targeting to bring an extra 75,000 tonnes of annual primary aluminium production onstream by the end of 2026. This involves reactivating potline one at their facility to increase output.

item.title: Steel rose to 3054 CNY/T on July 3, 2026, up 0.53% from the previous day
item.description: Steel prices increased to 3,054 CNY/T on July 3, 2026, marking a 0.53% rise from the prior day. Over the past month, steel prices have declined by 3.08%, though they remain 0.13% higher than the previous month's level[1].

item.title: The Boom in New Steel Mills Is Outpacing Demand
item.description: Twenty-one million tons of additional steel production capacity has been announced, placed under construction, or completed in the past four years, marking the most significant expansion of steelmaking capabilities seen in many years. The United States stands on the brink of a renewed era in steel production, but it now requires clientele to sustain this growth as manufacturers construct new facilities[5].

item.title: Nickel rose to 16,360 USD/T on July 3, 2026
item.description: Nickel prices rose to 16,360 USD/T on July 3, 2026, up 0.40% from the previous day. Over the past month, nickel's price has fallen 12.21%, but it remains 7.21% higher than the same period last year.

item.title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
item.description: Indonesia plans to cut nickel output in 2026 to support prices and has reversed its mining quota policy to regulate supply volatility. This strategic shift aims to stabilize the global nickel market amid fluctuating demand and production costs.

item.title: Nickel fell to $16,300 per tonne, its lowest level since late December, as Indonesia prepares to permit a substantial increase in nickel mine production
item.description: Nickel prices dropped to $16,300 per tonne, marking the lowest level since late December, as Indonesia signals it will allow a significant rise in nickel mine production. This anticipated increase in output is expected to further influence global supply dynamics and price trends.

item.title: Nickel Enters a New Era as Indonesia Tightens Supply
item.description: Nickel prices have surged past $1,000 per ton, a dramatic climb from $150 just 18 months ago, driven by Indonesia's tightening supply and rising production expenses. Globally, LME nickel inventories have dropped by 10,000 tons over the past two months, signaling a rapidly tightening market.

item.title: How could demand drive low carbon innovation in the steel industry
item.description: Scrap-based EAF steelmaking can reduce emissions by 90% if powered exclusively by renewable energy, emitting only 0.16 t CO2/t crude steel. Innovations supporting UK decarbonization include circular business models, material efficiency reducing demand by 40%, and increased renewable energy capacity.

item.title: Steel prices, news & market analysis
item.description: Latest market analysis highlights that US job growth halved in June to 57,000, impacting steel demand forecasts. Additionally, Japan steel groups have criticized new EU safeguard measures affecting trade flows[10].

item.title: Steel imports down 30% in 2026 as tariffs bolster US production
item.description: Section 232 tariffs have reduced foreign steel entering the United States by nearly 30% year-to-date in 2026, while domestic production has increased by nearly 5 million tons since the start of 2025. Although April saw a 6% monthly rise in imports, the overall trend supports domestic steel manufacturing amid disrupted trade flows.

item.title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions
item.description: U.S. steel manufacturers are planning near-term capacity additions to meet anticipated long-term growth, despite a recent slowdown in production levels. New production capacity is coming online as the industry looks toward positive global and domestic output forecasts for 2026.

item.title: Copper futures climbed toward $6.2 per pound on Friday and were on track for a weekly gain
item.description: Copper futures climbed toward $6.2 per pound on Friday as traders scaled back bets for Federal Reserve interest rate hikes, positioning for a weekly gain[6].

item.title: The Copper Mining Market in 2026: Current and Future Trends
item.description: Global copper mine production is expected to rise by 2.3% in 2026, driven by expansions in Chile, Peru, Zambia, and a rebound in Indonesia. However, refined copper production growth will decelerate to 0.9% due to limited concentrate supply, resulting in a projected refined copper deficit of 150,000 metric tons.

item.title: Industry Data - American Iron and Steel Institute
item.description: In the week ending July 4, 2026, domestic raw steel production reached 1,856,000 net tons, with a capability utilization rate of 80.4 percent. This data reflects strong operational performance and near-maximum capacity usage in the U.S. steel sector.

item.title: Global Copper Prices Expected To Rise, Major Markets ...
item.description: Projections indicate copper prices could reach $12,000 per ton by the end of 2026, a 22% surge compared to last year, driven by rising demand. The average price in 2025 was $9,947 per ton, highlighting the significant expected increase[3].

item.title: Italy's Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper tariffs
item.description: Milan-listed Prysmian, the world's largest cable maker, reported a 2.1% share increase in early trading following announcements of stronger U.S. pricing and margins due to new copper tariffs. Analysts at Citi noted that copper tariffs will support pricing as the company benefits from domestic copper procurement.

item.title: Aluminum closed in on prices not seen since 2022 following Iranian attacks on two Middle Eastern producers
item.description: Aluminum prices surged to levels not seen since 2022 after Iranian attacks targeted two major Middle Eastern producers over the weekend. The incident has intensified concerns about global supply stability and is expected to impact short-term market dynamics.

item.title: Is CENX's Higher Aluminum Production a Catalyst for Future Growth?
item.description: Century Aluminum produced approximately 638,000 tons of primary aluminum in 2025, with key operations at facilities like Grundartangi. Analysts question whether this increased production capacity could serve as a catalyst for the company's future growth trajectory.

item.title: 3 innovations in green steel
item.description: Boston Metal, a Bill Gates-backed startup, raised $50 million in January to develop a CO2-free steel process using molten oxide electrolysis. In Sweden, $3.99 billion is funding a new commercial hydrogen steel plant, while Boston Metal aims to commercialize MIT's technology over the next 2–3 years in regions with abundant renewable energy.

item.title: Green transformation in the iron and steel industry in India
item.description: Indian steel majors like SAIL, Tata Steel, and JSW Steel are actively pursuing R&D in carbon-cutting technologies, including Arcelor Mittal's SIDERWIN Technology, which offers up to 95% emission reduction compared to traditional methods. Senior executives are committed to sustainability, with India opening its first commercial green hydrogen plant in August 2022.

item.title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
item.description: Northern Sweden will produce the first commercial near-zero emissions steel, while innovators focus on 'Make Better' (direct electrification, feedstock innovations) and 'Make New' (disruptive low-emission processes). Companies like GreenOre and Boston Metal are monetizing steel slag waste streams to reduce pollution and accelerate commercial traction in cement and critical metals manufacturing.

item.title: Commerzbank: Mine production constraints support elevated copper prices
item.description: Commerzbank analysts warn that despite a projected 1.6% rise in mine production, ongoing constraints in Chile and Indonesia—where the Grasberg mine operates at only 40–50% capacity—continue to support elevated copper prices. The report highlights that mining operations remain the weakest link in global copper supply, with traders focusing on the $88,000 price level as a potential market bottom.

item.title: Copper Rally Gains Momentum as Supply Disruptions Drive Market Outlook
item.description: Copper prices are surging due to a recovery in Chinese demand and mounting concerns over global supply disruptions, particularly in mining sectors. Analysts note that resilient demand from the energy transition and data center infrastructure is creating a powerful tailwind for copper markets.

item.title: Development of $9bn copper and gold project pushed back as Middle East war adds to worsening local security situation
item.description: A major $9 billion copper and gold project has been delayed due to escalating security concerns linked to the Middle East war, which is worsening local instability. This development underscores the growing impact of geopolitical risks on large-scale copper capacity expansion.

item.title: Copper Archives - MINING.COM
item.description: Recent updates include Ottawa’s C$500M Red Chris investment testing British Columbia’s grid, LME approving Adani’s major copper smelter in India, and Canada committing $352M to Red Chris. These developments reflect ongoing efforts to expand copper capacity amid grid and permitting challenges.

item.title: Can nickel prices hit $25,000 in 2026?
item.description: Indonesia, producing two-thirds of the world's nickel, is slashing output quotas and delaying mining permits to support prices, revealing that much of the supposed 'paper surplus' never existed. While a clearing range of $20,000–$22,000/t is currently defensible, prices could test $25,000/t if supply continues to tighten.

item.title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
item.description: Nickel is critical for the net-zero transition with demand projected to grow 65% this decade, yet the industry must adopt more sustainable production methods to minimize environmental impact[3]. A meta-analysis confirms that clean energy technologies are the fastest-growing sector for nickel demand, making scalable, low-impact production imperative[3].

item.title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
item.description: The global nickel market is rapidly changing with new mining and refining innovations aimed at reducing production costs while enhancing environmental sustainability[8]. These advancements focus on making nickel production more efficient and less environmentally damaging to meet future demand[8].

item.title: Steel rose to 3058 CNY/T on July 6, 2026, up 0.13% from the previous day
item.description: Steel prices increased slightly to 3058 CNY per ton on July 6, 2026, marking a 0.13% rise from the prior day. Over the past month, steel prices have declined 2.49%, though they remain 1.09% higher than the previous year.

item.title: Steel Market Prices, Data, and Analysis
item.description: S&P Global offers comprehensive and timely information on steel, including historical and spot prices, along with real-time data for market participants. Their analysis supports informed decision-making in the global steel market.

item.title: Copper Price - Copper Rate in India Today Live on The ...
item.description: Copper prices are surging globally due to geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and booming AI-driven demand, with the metal increasingly viewed as a strategic commodity for the AI era. In India, copper prices rose nearly 1% to Rs 1,089.20/kg, tracking international gains ahead of the US Federal Reserve’s policy decision.

item.title: UAE aluminum giant EGA could bring US its first new smelter since 1980
item.description: Emirates Global Aluminum plans to invest $4 billion to develop a primary production plant in Inola, Oklahoma, expected to be operational by the end of 2026. The facility will have the capacity to produce 600,000 tons of primary aluminum and create 1,000 jobs.

item.title: Nucor holds spot HR price at $1,130/ton, again
item.description: Nucor announced on Monday, July 6, that its hot-rolled coil consumer spot price will remain unchanged at $1,130 per ton. This decision reflects stability in the current steel market pricing for consumer spot transactions.

item.title: U.S. Steel Production Has Surpassed Japan for the First Time in Nearly Three Decades
item.description: The United States has ranked third globally in steel production, behind China and India, marking a significant shift in global steel manufacturing dynamics. This milestone indicates the U.S. has surpassed Japan, a leader it had not overtaken in nearly 30 years.

item.title: Reducing the carbon footprint thanks to greener steel production
item.description: ArcelorMittal is piloting clean-power steelmaking and circular low-emission carbon steelmaking to enhance sustainability. Jérôme Favero highlights that lighter vehicles using smart steel solutions and innovative design are the most efficient way to reduce CO2 emissions today.

item.title: Major Milestone Achieved in Steel Sector with Innovations and Sustainability
item.description: A new project demonstrates a viable path to sustainable steel and sets a precedent for nations aiming for carbon neutrality. The milestone highlights the integration of innovation and sustainability in the steel sector.

item.title: The future of steel is here: Innovation meets sustainability as Sandvik leads the way
item.description: Sandvik has produced the industry's first dump box using SSAB steel, showcasing how innovation meets sustainability. This milestone underscores SSAB's leadership in advancing low-emission steel applications.

item.title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
item.description: This article investigates how optimizing nickel production can create a sustainable global battery industry by implementing technologies that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve energy efficiency. It emphasizes that enhancing stakeholder engagement and robust ESG disclosure standards are essential for transparency in the nickel sector.

item.title: Italian steelmaker Cogne Acciai Speciali sourcing all energy from renewables
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali now sources all of its power needs in Europe from renewable energy, with plans to build a hydroelectric plant and electrolyzer at its main Italian site in Aosta. This move underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and reducing its environmental footprint.

item.title: Steel fell to 3,060 CNY/T on July 7, 2026, down 0.10% from the previous day
item.description: Steel prices dropped slightly to 3,060 CNY per ton on July 7, 2026, marking a 0.10% decline from the prior day. Over the past month, steel prices have fallen 2.42%, though they remain 0.82% higher than the previous year.

item.title: Copper Prices Hit Record High After US Imposes Tariffs
item.description: Copper prices reached a new record high after the US announced copper tariffs aligned with existing 50% tariffs on steel and aluminum. A bullish Goldman Sachs report indicates that copper demand in China will increase by 6% in 2025 despite these market shifts.

item.title: Copper fell to 6.16 USD/Lbs on July 7, 2026
item.description: Copper prices fell to 6.16 USD/Lbs on July 7, 2026, down 0.32% from the previous day. Over the past month, the price has fallen 2.71%, though it remains 9.09% higher than the same period last year.

item.title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
item.description: Copper's long-term outlook is constrained by the industry's limited ability to bring new supply online fast enough to meet rising demand. Permitting delays and slow project lead times continue to limit the speed of new supply integration.

item.title: Nickel fell to 16347 USD/T on July 7, 2026, down 0.51% from the previous day
item.description: Nickel prices dropped to $16,347 per ton on July 7, 2026, marking a 0.51% decline from the previous day. Over the past month, the metal has fallen 11.16%, though it remains 8.87% higher than the same period last year.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today
item.description: Nickel futures are currently trading at $16,342.25, down from a previous close of $16,420.88. The spot price for nickel today is reported at $16,392.38.
